summary: Excellent audience recording captures a scorching 1982 Minnesota show with smoking Scarlet > Fire and a 21‑song marathon.
review: Fans rave about the front‑row feel of this audience tape, noting its superb instrumental balance despite a slight boominess and the need to boost Phil’s bass a touch. The performance is described as a “smoking” night with standout moments like Scarlet > Fire on the Mountain, Candyman, Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance and an inspiring Space jam that keep the energy high throughout the 21‑song set.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating ≈ 4.8 across 13 reviews, uniformly praising both musical performance and audio quality; consistent positive language indicates a strong show with excellent recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑row audience mic (behind the stage) gives crisp, balanced sound with a slight boominess and occasional bass being buried; reviewers suggest turning Phil up a bit but overall the tape sounds clear and immersive.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: A 21‑song monster that kept the crowd sweating; seamless transitions (Scarlet > Fire, drums into Space) and high energy from start to finish are repeatedly highlighted.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Powerful vocal inflections on Candyman and sizzling guitar work during Fire on the Mountain; his “outer‑space” themes noted in the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass sometimes buried in early ’80s board sound, prompting suggestions to boost his level; praised for strong tone and leading the transition from Scarlet into Fire on the Mountain.
band_member_comments.Drums: Jam before drums and the drum‑to‑Space transition are singled out as favorite early‑’80s mix‑up moments.
